channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Baidu Cloud Upgrades to Full-Stack AI Cloud for Agent Era at Create 2026 Conference
item.description: At Create 2026, Baidu announced a comprehensive upgrade of Baidu Cloud to an agent-centric full-stack AI cloud, featuring the Token Factory for agent-first inference, KV Cache hit rates exceeding 90%, and the Kunlun P800 completing multiple 10,000-GPU clusters at 97% training efficiency.

item.title: Nokia Rolls Out Agentic AI for Fixed Networks, Targeting $6.2B Telecom Market by 2030
item.description: Nokia announced agentic AI capabilities across its Altiplano, Corteca, and Broadband Easy platforms, enabling autonomous network troubleshooting, field technician support, and self-optimizing infrastructure with a projected $6.2B telecom investment by 2030.

item.title: China's 'Jiuzhang 4' Quantum Computer Achieves 10^54 Speedup Over Supercomputers
item.description: Researchers from USTC have built 'Jiuzhang 4,' a programmable quantum computing prototype with 8,176 modes and 1,024 squeezed state inputs, achieving a quantum advantage ratio of 10^54 over the world's fastest supercomputer El Capitan.

item.title: SenseTime Launches Embodied AI Convenience Store with Robotic Staff in Shanghai
item.description: SenseTime's latest 'Shaomai Gou' embodied AI convenience store debuted in Shanghai during the May Day holiday, with robotic arms handling orders in as fast as 15 seconds and handling 400 daily transactions without human staff.

item.title: Shanghai Xingji Power Technologies: Nuclear Fusion Derivative Tech Lands in Shanghai Metro
item.description: While commercial fusion power remains decades away, technologies spawned from 'artificial sun' research are already appearing in everyday life — from Hefei Metro's touchless security scanners to plasma thrusters for ultra-low orbit satellites.

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard and API service issues
item.description: May 14, 00:29 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 13, 23:53 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues affecting the Load Balancer API and dashboard, along with delays in audit log delivery for some services. Customers may experience failed or delayed requests to the Load...

item.title: INC0157155
item.description: May 14, 00:19 UTCResolved - Current status: After investigating, we've determined this to be a false positive. There were no customer replication refresh operations impacted. We've confirmed replication services are functioning normally. If you experience issues or have questions, please open a...

item.title: Issues accessing ChatGPT for logged-out users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Login (Operational)

item.title: Browser Run API availability issues
item.description: May 13, 20:50 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 13, 20:47 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 13, 20:22 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare has identified the issue and is implementing a fix. We will update the status once we implement the fix.May...

item.title: AI chatbots are giving out people’s real phone numbers
item.description: People report that their personal contact info was surfaced by Google AI—and there’s apparently no easy way to prevent it.  A Redditor recently wrote that he was “desperate for help”: for about a month, he said, his phone had been inundated by calls from “strangers” who were “looking for a...

item.title: Foxconn Confirms North American Factories Hit by Cyberattack
item.description: The Nitrogen ransomware group claims to have hacked the company’s systems, stealing 8TB of data, including confidential documents. The post Foxconn Confirms North American Factories Hit by Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Incident with CodeQL
item.description: May 13, 16:03 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.May 13, 15:30 UTCUpdate - CodeQL impact has been mitigated. We are continuing to monitor...

item.title: Microsoft, Palo Alto Networks Find Many Vulnerabilities by Using AI on Their Own Code
item.description: Microsoft’s MDASH discovered 16 of the Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities, and Palo Alto used Mythos to find dozens of flaws. The post Microsoft, Palo Alto Networks Find Many Vulnerabilities by Using AI on Their Own Code appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cache Purging and Cache Rule Update Issues
item.description: May 13, 15:26 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 13, 15:24 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue affecting cache purge requests for Cache Reserve customers and updates to cache-related zone settings. Affected customers may see failures or timeouts when attempting to...

item.title: Increased HTTP 502 Errors in Hong Kong region
item.description: May 13, 15:08 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 13, 14:49 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 502 errors impacting customers in Hong Kong region

item.title: Sweet Security Launches Agentic AI Red Teaming to Counter ‘Mythos Moment’
item.description: New “Sweet Attack” platform uses runtime intelligence and continuous agentic red teaming to identify exploitable attack chains human teams may miss. The post Sweet Security Launches Agentic AI Red Teaming to Counter ‘Mythos Moment’ appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Errors with Email Sending via the Worker Binding
item.description: May 13, 14:39 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Email Sending via the Worker Binding where an error message "Invalid email address: Invalid input" can be returned if the recipient list is an array.More updates to follow soon.

item.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview. MDASH, short for multi-model agentic scanning...

item.title: Webinar Today: ROI for Cyber-Physical Security Programs
item.description: This webinar will help OT security teams and asset owners stop being cost centers and start being resilience drivers. The post Webinar Today: ROI for Cyber-Physical Security Programs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
item.description: A threat actor with affiliations to China has been linked to a "multi-wave intrusion" targeting an unnamed Azerbaijani oil and gas company between late December 2025 and late February 2026, marking an expansion of its targeting. The activity has been attributed by Bitdefender with moderate-to-high...

item.title: Claude.ai is experiencing elevated error rates
item.description: May 13, 12:59 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.May 13, 12:21 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Government to Scrutinize Instructure Over Canvas Disruption, Data Breach
item.description: The Committee on Homeland Security has requested to be briefed on the incident and Instructure’s remediation steps. The post Government to Scrutinize Instructure Over Canvas Disruption, Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: making drugs in orbit and NASA’s nuclear-powered spacecraft
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A plan to make drugs in orbit is going commercial A startup called Varda Space Industries is betting that the future of pharmaceuticals lies in...

item.title: Claude.ai is experiencing elevated error rates
item.description: May 13, 11:57 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 13, 11:48 UTCMonitoring - We are seeing recovery and currently monitoring at this time.May 13, 11:25 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: [Webinar] Why Your AppSec Tools Miss the "Lethal Path" (and How to Fix It)
item.description: TL;DR: Stop chasing thousands of "toast" alerts. Join experts from Wiz and Okta/GitLab to learn how hackers connect tiny flaws to build a "Lethal Chain" to your data—and how to break it. Register for the Strategic Briefing Here. Most security tools work like a smoke alarm that goes off every time...

item.title: Most Remediation Programs Never Confirm the Fix Actually Worked
item.description: Security teams have never had better visibility into their environments and never been worse at confirming what they fix stays fixed. Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report puts the mean time to exploit at an estimated negative seven days. The Verizon 2025 DBIR puts median time to remediate edge device...

item.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities spanning its product portfolio, although none of them have been listed as publicly known or under active attack. Of the 138 flaws, 30 are rated Critical, 104 are rated Important, three are rated Moderate, and one is rated Low in...

item.title: Codex 5.5 engines are experiencing high error rate
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Codex Web (Operational) CLI (Operational) App (Operational) VS Code extension (Operational) Codex API (Operational)

item.title: A plan to make drugs in orbit is going commercial
item.description: Varda Space Industries, a startup that’s been pitching its ability to perform drug experiments in space, says it has signed up the pharmaceutical company United Therapeutics in what may be remembered as a notable step toward in-orbit manufacturing. The idea of building things in outer space for use...

item.title: Realtime API - SIP/WebRTC flow are down
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components Audio (Degraded performance)

item.title: A Cascaded Generative Approach for e-Commerce Recommendations
item.description: arXiv:2605.11118v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Personalized storefronts in large e-commerce marketplaces are often assembled from many independent components: static themes per page section ("placement"), retrieval systems to fetch eligible products per placement, and pointwise rankers to order...

item.title: EVOCHAMBER: Test-Time Co-evolution of Multi-Agent System at Individual, Team, and Population Scales
item.description: arXiv:2605.11136v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We argue that multi-agent test-time evolution is not single-agent evolution replicated N times. A single-agent learner can only evolve its own context and memory. A multi-agent system additionally evolves who collaborates, how they collaborate, and...

item.title: RankQ: Offline-to-Online Reinforcement Learning via Self-Supervised Action Ranking
item.description: arXiv:2605.11151v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline-to-online reinforcement learning (RL) improves sample efficiency by leveraging pre-collected datasets prior to online interaction. A key challenge, however, is learning an accurate critic in large state--action spaces with limited dataset...

item.title: OLIVIA: Online Learning via Inference-time Action Adaptation for Decision Making in LLM ReAct Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.11169v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model agents interleave reasoning, action selection, and observation to solve sequential decision-making tasks. In deployed settings where agents repeatedly handle related multi-step tasks, small action-selection errors can accumulate...

item.title: The Many Faces of On-Policy Distillation: Pitfalls, Mechanisms, and Fixes
item.description: arXiv:2605.11182v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: On-policy distillation (OPD) and on-policy self-distillation (OPSD) have emerged as promising post-training methods for large language models, offering dense token-level supervision on trajectories sampled from the model's own policy. However,...

item.title: Interpretable EEG Microstate Discovery via Variational Deep Embedding: A Systematic Architecture Search with Multi-Quadrant Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2605.10947v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: EEG microstate analysis segments continuous brain electrical activity into brief, quasi-stable topographic configurations that reflect discrete functional brain states. Conventional approaches such as Modified K-Means operate directly in electrode...

item.title: QuIDE: Mastering the Quantized Intelligence Trade-off via Active Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.10959v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: There is currently no unified metric for evaluating the efficiency of quantized neural networks. We propose QuIDE, built around the Intelligence Index I = (C x P)/log_2(T+1), which collapses the compression-accuracy-latency trade-off into a single...

item.title: Steering Without Breaking: Mechanistically Informed Interventions for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.10971v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Discrete diffusion language models (DLMs) generate text by iteratively denoising all positions in parallel, offering an alternative to autoregressive models. Controlled generation methods for DLMs, imported from autoregressive models, apply uniform...

item.title: Rotation-Preserving Supervised Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2605.10973v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) improves in-domain performance but can degrade out-of-domain (OOD) generalization. Prior work suggests that this degradation is related to changes in dominant singular subspaces of pretrained weight matrices. However,...

item.title: Vertex-Softmax: Tight Transformer Verification via Exact Softmax Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.10974v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Certified verification of transformer attention requires bounding the softmax function over interval constraints on the pre-softmax scores. Existing verifiers relax softmax ndependently of the downstream objective, leaving avoidable slack. We prove...

item.title: Sampling More, Getting Less: Calibration is the Diversity Bottleneck in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2605.11128v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diversity is essential for language-model applications ranging from creative generation to scientific discovery, yet modern LLMs often collapse into a narrow subset of plausible outputs. While prior work has developed benchmarks for measuring this...

item.title: ClinicalBench: Stress-Testing Assertion-Aware Retrieval for Cross-Admission Clinical QA on MIMIC-IV
item.description: arXiv:2605.11143v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reasoning benchmarks measure clinical performance on clean inputs. We evaluate the step before reasoning: retrieval over real EHR notes, where negation, temporality, and family-versus-patient attribution can flip a correct answer to a wrong one. EpiKG...

item.title: Decomposing Evolutionary Mixture-of-LoRA Architectures: The Routing Lever, the Lifecycle Penalty, and a Substrate-Conditional Boundary
item.description: arXiv:2605.11153v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We decompose an evolutionary mixture-of-LoRA system on a from-scratch ~150M-parameter widened-D substrate (D=1536, V=32000; D/V approx 0.048; the "widened-1536" substrate) into three factors -- a router rewrite (parallel sigmoid gate with learnable...

item.title: The Bicameral Model: Bidirectional Hidden-State Coupling Between Parallel Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.11167v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing multi-model and tool-augmented systems communicate by generating text, serializing every exchange through the output vocabulary. Can two pretrained language models instead coordinate through a continuous, concurrent channel? The Bicameral...

item.title: How Does Differential Privacy Affect Social Bias in LLMs? A Systematic Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2605.11195v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) trained on web-scale corpora can memorize sensitive training data, posing significant privacy risks. Differential privacy (DP) has emerged as a principled framework that limits the influence of individual data points...

item.title: [AINews] The End of Finetuning
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on whither finetuning

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 12, 23:58 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 12, 23:51 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 12, 23:38 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
item.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers --...

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5
item.description: May 12, 20:13 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 12, 19:36 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating elevated errors on requests to Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: Elevated Error Rate for Vaults and Credentials
item.description: May 12, 18:57 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 12, 18:51 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 12, 18:47 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.
